text: Cyclic structures consisting of linked rings are frequently found in a large number of important biological natural and synthetic molecules. Those consisting of two or more rings, or consisting of a ring combined with other functional groups, are of particular interest, and are commonly referred to as benzannulated medium-sized and macrocyclic ring (BMR) systems. This class includes a wide variety of significant structures such as steroids, polypeptides, anthracyclins, various types of porphyrins and other heterocyclic macrocycles as well as cyclic peptides, saccharides and tetrapeptides.
explanation: No obvious AI-generated markers; the text is coherent and domain-specific. A unfamiliar acronym (BMR) and some phrasing quirks could hint at automated drafting, but it’s plausible human science writing.
